transcript.clean_text: day two of the tour of a man from Sultan Qaboos University - al burst on a very different profile today number one with some difficult climbs along the way with much warmer temperatures the pace was hot right from the start of the stage for intrepid breakaway men Loic cheto of coffee dias for Bridger Ferrari of codger Royale from Uruguay Danny pate of rally cycling of Marco IRA's our of Trek Sega Fredo as the climbs have reached the former under-23 a world champion of the time trial pate struggled three riders continued on their attack of the day IRA's are was keen to keep pushing the pace one of the oldest riders in the field was in his 15th season as a professional rider BMC Racing team were looking strong for the entire day reeling in a cheto of kapha discs and then finally catching IRA's are on yet another climb the red jersey guard struggled Astana started to push the pace five kilometres of racing to go over the top of algea and then all downhill as 14 riders went clear BMC Racing team tried to lead out Greg Van a vomit into the final kilometer of racing as the Sprint opened up the Olympic champion looked absolutely superb but it was Nathan house who had done his homework pouncing on the right-hand-side to take the win an emotional Nathan house the Australian taking a huge victory and now he leads the race overall he'll carry the red jersey into stage three
